standards_and_methodology: The "GCCA EPD Tool" is a specific tool to the cement and concrete industry, it is compliant with the EN 15804+A2 standard and is verified by EPD international. The allocation rules defined by EN 15804+A2 and EN16908 have been applied.
lca_software_and_database: For the calculation of the LCA results, the software program "GCCA EPD Tool" (v4.0) was used for A1 to A3 calculations and is based on ecoinvent 3.5. The "GCCA EPD Tool" is a specific tool to the cement and concrete industry, it is compliant with the EN 15804+A2 standard and is verified by EPD international. SimaPro was used to model module A4 module in combination with ecoinvent v3.8. Background data is included in the GCCA EPD tool and has been verified by EPD International. Ecoinvent v3.5 for A1 to A3 and ecoinvent version 3.8 for A4.

production_sites: The EPD is specific to the product and manufacturing site. The data used for the LCA are specific for this product which is manufactured by a single manufacturer in a single production site.
cut_off_criteria:
The following processes are considered below cut-off:
- Cleaning of the installation
- Wear of grinding balls
- Cement grinding losses
- Cement mixing losses

Following processes were excluded for the inventory:
- Production and construction of equipment for buildings and installations
- Production and construction of equipment for the production of clinker and cement and lab analyses
- Activities related to the personnel (mobility, furniture and desk consumables)
- Utilization of water and energy related to the sales and management of the product
- Cleaning of offices
allocation:
The allocation rules defined by EN 15804+A2 and EN16908 have been applied.
The manufacturing process does not produce co-products that require allocation.
A cut-off is applied when recyclable waste reaches the end of the waste state.
By default, this EPD addresses the environmental impacts of cement production excluding the incineration of the input waste (net figure). For clarity and transparency, the gross figure is also communicated as recommended in the EN 16908+A1.
For the blast furnace slag, a co-product of the pig iron production, economic allocation has been used conform EN 15804+A2, based on EPD data from the main supplier.
For the fly ash, a waste from coal-fired power plants, no allocated has been accounted for in this EPD.
data_quality:
The activity data is based on site specific data (volumes, lower heating values, emission factors) verified by the EU ETS system for the clinker production. For the cement production, the main data used is the recipe.
Background data is included in the GCCA EPD tool and has been verified by EPD International.
time_representativeness: Manufacturer specific data have been collected for the year 2021.
geographical_representativeness: The residual electricity mix of Belgium in 2021 was used, based on the electricity attributes provided by the Association of issuing Bodies (AIB).
impact_assessment: The EF3.0 version of the EN 15804+A2 LCIA method was used. The characterization factors from EC-JRC were applied. No additional or deviating characterisation factors were used.
additional_paragraphs: The product does not contain biogenic carbon. The product is sold in bulk and does not require packaging. Biogenic carbon content in product (at the gate): 0 kg C / FU. Biogenic carbon content in accompanying packaging (at the gate): 0 kg C / FU.
Carbon offsetting is not allowed in the EN 15804+A2 and hence not taken into account in the calculations
No carbonation of cementitious materials is considered in the scope of this EPD. The construction products made with cement (mortar, concrete) may indeed carbonate in the following life cycle stages.
The EPD is specific to the product and manufacturing site. No underlying parameters or assumptions that would impact the results were made. The results do not vary.

subsections.title: Software
subsections.content: For the calculation of the LCA results, the software program "GCCA EPD Tool" (v4.0) was used for A1 to A3 calculations and is based on ecoinvent 3.5. The "GCCA EPD Tool" is a specific tool to the cement and concrete industry, it is compliant with the EN 15804+A2 standard and is verified by EPD international. SimaPro was used to model module A4 module in combination with ecoinvent v3.8 .

subsections.title: Information on allocation
subsections.content:
The allocation rules defined by EN 15804+A2 and EN16908 have been applied.
The manufacturing process does not produce co-products that require allocation.
A cut-off is applied when recyclable waste reaches the end of the waste state.
By default, this EPD addresses the environmental impacts of cement production excluding the incineration of the input waste (net figure). For clarity and transparency, the gross figure is also communicated as recommended in the EN 16908+A1.
For the blast furnace slag, a co-product of the pig iron production, economic allocation has been used conform EN 15804+A2, based on EPD data from the main supplier.
For the fly ash, a waste from coal-fired power plants, no allocated has been accounted for in this EPD.

subsections.title: Information on cut off
subsections.content:
The following processes are considered below cut-off:
- Cleaning of the installation
- Wear of grinding balls
- Cement grinding losses
- Cement mixing losses

subsections.title: Information on excluded processes
subsections.content:
Following processes were excluded for the inventory:
- Production and construction of equipment for buildings and installations
- Production and construction of equipment for the production of clinker and cement and lab analyses
- Activities related to the personnel (mobility, furniture and desk consumables)
- Utilization of water and energy related to the sales and management of the product
- Cleaning of offices

subsections.title: Information on biogenic carbon modelling
subsections.content:
The product does not contain biogenic carbon.
The product is sold in bulk and does not require packaging.
Biogenic carbon content in product (at the gate): 0 kg C / FU
Biogenic carbon content in accompanying packaging (at the gate): 0 kg C / FU

subsections.title: Information on carbon offsetting
subsections.content: Carbon offsetting is not allowed in the EN 15804+A2 and hence not taken into account in the calculations

subsections.title: Information on carbonation of cementitious materials
subsections.content: No carbonation of cementitious materials is considered in the scope of this EPD. The construction products made with cement (mortar, concrete) may indeed carbonate in the following life cycle stages.

subsections.title: Additional or deviating characterisation factors
subsections.content: The EF3.0 version of the EN 15804+A2 LCIA method was used. The characterization factors from EC-JRC were applied. No additional or deviating characterisation factors were used.

subsections.title: Description of the variability
subsections.content: The EPD is specific to the product and manufacturing site. No underlying parameters or assumptions that would impact the results were made. The results do not vary.

subsections.title: Specificity
subsections.content: The data used for the LCA are specific for this product which is manufactured by a single manufacturer in a single production site.

subsections.title: Period of data collection
subsections.content: Manufacturer specific data have been collected for the year 2021.

subsections.title: Information on data collection
subsections.content:
The activity data is based on site specific data (volumes, lower heating values, emission factors) verified by the EU ETS system for the clinker production. For the cement production, the main data used is the recipe.
Background data is included in the GCCA EPD tool and has been verified by EPD International.

subsections.title: Database used for background data
subsections.content: Ecoinvent v3.5 for A1 to A3 and ecoinvent version 3.8 for A4.

subsections.title: Energy mix
subsections.content: The residual electricity mix of Belgium in 2021 was used, based on the electricity attributes provided by the Association of issuing Bodies (AIB).
